notes.content: Environmental engineers are responsible for designing processes and infrastructure to ensure society has access to safe water, clean air, and healthy ecosystems. Environmental engineers apply knowledge from physics, chemistry, biology and the social sciences to problems in a variety of areas including water & wastewater treatment and ecosystem remediation, analysis of chemical fate and transport in the natural environment, and modeling of hydrologic & atmospheric flows. As climate change creates new challenges, such as in the form of droughts and intense weather events, the field of environmental engineering evolves to meet society’s needs. As an environmental engineering student at UC Davis, you will gain skills that enable you to design sustainable solutions for society. The Environmental Engineering Bachelor of Science is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Environmental Engineering and Similarly Named Engineering Programs.
notes.contentHtml:
Environmental engineers are responsible for designing processes and infrastructure to ensure society has access to safe water, clean air, and healthy ecosystems. Environmental engineers apply knowledge from physics, chemistry, biology and the social sciences to problems in a variety of areas including water & wastewater treatment and ecosystem remediation, analysis of chemical fate and transport in the natural environment, and modeling of hydrologic & atmospheric flows. As climate change creates new challenges, such as in the form of droughts and intense weather events, the field of environmental engineering evolves to meet society’s needs. As an environmental engineering student at UC Davis, you will gain skills that enable you to design sustainable solutions for society.


The Environmental Engineering Bachelor of Science is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Environmental Engineering and Similarly Named Engineering Programs.

notes.content: This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students need to meet a minimum 3.10 GPA cumulative and a 3.20 GPA in major preparation coursework. All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements. Important Information: Complete the courses below: ● Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B ● Organic Chemistry (CHE) 008A ● Engineering (ENG) 006 or Engineering Computer Science (ECS) 032A ● Engineering (ENG) 035 ● Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D ● Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B ● Physics (PHY) 009A/B Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB .
notes.contentHtml:
This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students need to meet a minimum 3.10 GPA cumulative and a 3.20 GPA in major preparation coursework.


All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements.


Important Information:


Complete the courses below:

●    Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B

●    Organic Chemistry (CHE) 008A

●    Engineering (ENG) 006 or Engineering Computer Science (ECS) 032A

●    Engineering (ENG) 035

●    Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D

●    Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B

●    Physics (PHY) 009A/B


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: At the time of TAG submission, the College of Engineering requires a minimum 3.50 cumulative GPA and at least a 3.50 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.

notes.content: Group 9: ECI 003 is designed for lower division. Transfer students will take ECI 101 post-matriculation if they have not taken ECI 003.
